# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create Kubernetes deployment manifests for microservices. Add annotations to control Istio sidecar injection behavior. Set sidecar.istio.io/inject to manage proxy injection per deployment. This is for our service mesh deployment strategy.
# Category: istio_sidecar_injection_disabled

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: microservice-deployment
spec:
  replicas: {{ .Values.replicaCount }}   # default value is set in values_default.yml or provided as a parameter when running the command line tool (e.g., kubectl apply -f deployment.yaml)
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: microservice-app     # matches with labels defined on pods, e.g.: {"app": "microservices"} in a Kubernetes Pod definition file or the command line tool (e.g., kubectl get pods)
  template:  
    metadata:      
      annotations:          # Annotation to control Istio sidecar injection behavior, e.g.: {"sidecar-inject": "true"} in a Kubernetes Pod definition file or the command line tool (e.g., kubectl get pods) 
        istio-injection: {{ .Values.istioInjection }}   # default value set to true, can be overridden by providing parameter when running the command like "kubectl apply -f deployment.yaml --set=istioInjection=false" or using values_default.yml
      labels:                # Labels for identifying pods and services in Kubernetes cluster 
        app: microservice-app   # matches with selector defined on deployments, e.g.: {"app": "microservices"} when running the command line tool (e.g., kubectl get pods) or using values_default.yml file to set labels in deployment yaml 
    spec:      
      containers:           # Container definition for each microservice, e.g.: {"name": "microservices", ...} when running the command line tool (e.g., kubectl get pods) or using values_default.yml file to set container in deployment yaml 
        - name: {{ .Values.containerName }}   # default value is provided by template, can be overridden as per requirement when running the command line tool (e.g., kubectl apply -f my-deployment.yaml --set=image=my/microservices) 
          image: "{{ .Values.containerImage }}"   # default value is provided by template, can be overridden as per requirement when running the command line tool (e.g., kubectl apply -f my-deployment.yaml --set=image=my/microservices) 
          ports:                # Ports definition for each microservice in Kubernetes cluster  
            - containerPort: {{ .Values.containerPort }}    # default value is provided by template, can be overridden as per requirement when running the command line tool (e.g., kubectl apply -f my-deployment.yaml --set=imagePorts[0].port=80)
